cards.title: lowercase n: The Foundation
cards.body: Draw n before almost everything else. Its stem weight, arch shape, and counter proportions are the DNA for h, m, u, and r — and indirectly for nearly every other lowercase letter.
tips: Start with a rectangle for stem width.
Draw arch as separate overlapping shape.
Counter affects readability at text sizes.
Duplicate first stem for second leg.

cards.title: lowercase o: The Curve Master
cards.body: The lowercase o is the curve master. Its bowl, stress axis, and stroke weights are reused in c, e, b, d, p, q, and g. Get o right before drawing any of those.
tips: Requires overshoot at top and bottom.
Stress axis must be consistent across curves.
Do not just scale down uppercase O.
Bowl weight = n crossbar weight in low contrast.

cards.title: lowercase h: Extension from n
cards.body: h = n with the left stem extended to the ascender line. Everything below x-height is identical to n.
tips: Arch begins at same height as n.
Serif at top should match l and b ascenders.
Ascender height must match l exactly.

cards.title: lowercase m: Doubled Rhythm
cards.body: m = n doubled. Copy arch and second leg from n, duplicate it rightward. Three stems must be identical weight.
tips: Counters should feel optically equal to n counter.
Widen letter if counters feel pinched.

cards.title: lowercase u: The Light Catcher
cards.body: u is not a mirror of n. The open top catches more light, making u appear wider. Narrow the bowl ~5-8 units for balance.
tips: Bottom curve needs overshoot like o.
Exit stroke top-right matches n arch exit.
Transition from stem to curve must be smooth.

cards.title: lowercase l: The Standard Stem
cards.body: l is your pure stem. Width, weight, and terminal treatment define DNA for i, j, b, d, h, k, p.
tips: Foot/serif treatment must match all ascending letters.
Side bearings are reference for uprights.

cards.title: lowercase i: Stem and Tittle
cards.body: i = l stem + tittle (dot). Tittle size requires careful optical calibration.
tips: Tittle larger than feels right — check at small sizes.
Position between x-height and ascender line.

cards.title: lowercase j: Descending Hook
cards.body: j = i + descending hook. Hook style should match f, r, and c terminal vocabulary.
tips: Hook style matches your curved terminals.

cards.title: lowercase b: Stem and Bowl
cards.body: b = h stem + o bowl. Not a mirror of d. Bowl attaches at x-height and baseline.

cards.title: lowercase d: Ascender and Bowl
cards.body: d = o bowl + ascender stem. Source for single-story a.
tips: Ascender height matches l and h.

cards.title: lowercase p: Descender and Bowl
cards.body: p = b bowl + descending stem. Stem descends below baseline.
tips: Descender length matches g, j, q, y.

cards.title: lowercase q: The Tail Tailor
cards.body: q = o bowl + descending stem. Source for single-story g.
tips: Tail matches j and g descender style.

cards.title: lowercase c: Opening the Circle
cards.body: c = o opened on right. Terminal flows into e, f, r.
tips: Aperture width is a stylistic signature.

cards.title: lowercase e: Crossbar Stability
cards.body: e = c + crossbar. Crossbar slightly below vertical center.
tips: Upper bowl smaller than lower bowl.

cards.title: lowercase f: Hook and Crossbar
cards.body: f = j reversed + crossbar. Crossbar height aligns with t and e.
tips: Top arc matches r and c vocabulary.

cards.title: lowercase t: The Short Ascender
cards.body: t = l stem + crossbar. Short ascender, aligns with f.
tips: Stem height below full ascender line.

cards.title: lowercase r: Arm and Terminal
cards.body: r = n without second leg. Arm terminal is a major personality statement.
tips: Terminal type (round, flat, angled) defines vocabulary.
Keep counter under the arm open.

cards.title: lowercase s: The Spine
cards.body: Copy c, mirror bottom. Spine carries same visual weight as thick strokes.
tips: Use two o guides for optical center.
Spine angle steep = modern; flat = soft.
Top lobe smaller than bottom lobe.

cards.title: lowercase a: Double Story Devil
cards.body: Mirror s vertically for body. Use d bowl and q stem.
tips: Overhang (roof) is difficult — defined by angle/curve.
Bowl matches d and q shapes.
Ear echoes r arm or c terminal vocabulary.

cards.title: lowercase g: Loop Specialist
cards.body: Single-story g (q + terminal) or double-story (o + s).
tips: Ear matches r and c family.
Double-story link is thin stroke weight.
Lower loop matches other descender depths.

cards.title: lowercase v: Diagonal Foundation
cards.body: Sets diagonal angle for w, y, x, k. Heavy left, thin right.
tips: Tapering creates extra visual space in bearings.

cards.title: lowercase w: Doubled v
cards.body: w = doubled v. Narrow the center slightly for balance.
tips: Outer arms match v angle exactly.
Check rhythm in text — w can be heavy.

cards.title: lowercase x: Optical Crossing
cards.body: Cross slightly ABOVE center. Thin/inktrap at the junction.
tips: Balance the four surrounding counter spaces.

cards.title: lowercase y: Diagonal Loop
cards.body: y = v top + descending tail. Match j and g vocabulary.
tips: Transition from arm to tail must be smooth.
Left arm is heavy, right arm thin.

cards.title: lowercase z: Diagonal Thinning
cards.body: Establish diagonal angle. Thick stroke is the diagonal.
tips: Diagonal thickening is severe; measure it.
Terminals match C and S style.

cards.title: lowercase k: Counter Balance
cards.body: k = l stem + z diagonals. Upper thin, lower thick.
tips: Three white spaces visually equal in area.
Diagonal junction is major stylistic indicator.

cards.title: uppercase H: Capital Anchor
cards.body: Primary capital foundation. Uprights and crossbar define DNA for 75% of caps.
tips: Side bearing is reference for upright capitals.

cards.title: uppercase O: Curve Anchor
cards.body: Secondary foundation letter. All cap curves derive from O (C, G, Q, D).

cards.title: uppercase I: The Cap Stem
cards.body: I = single stem from H. Add serifs in sans-serif for legibility.

cards.title: uppercase L: The Cap Foot
cards.body: L = I + foot. Foot length determines white space pocket.
tips: Foot terminal match T and E crossbar ends.
Base structure for E and F.

cards.title: uppercase T: The Cap Crossbar
cards.body: T = I + top crossbar. Crossbar lighter than upright.
tips: Terminal style matches L and E.
Test against E and F heights.

cards.title: uppercase E: Balanced Arms
cards.body: E = L + two crossbars. Middle arm is shorter and above center.
tips: Optical stability: middle arm slightly above center.

cards.title: uppercase F: High Point
cards.body: F = E minus bottom arm. Middle arm may need shift.
tips: Watch for top-heaviness after removing bottom arm.
Check middle arm position for visual balance.

cards.title: uppercase C: Open Cap Bowl
cards.body: C = O opened on right. Structure flows into G and S.

cards.title: uppercase D: Cap Bowl Stability
cards.body: D = H stem + O right half. Flatten the bowl slightly for stability.
tips: Junctions top and bottom must be invisible.

cards.title: uppercase B: Visual Centers
cards.body: B has two bowls. Bottom bowl larger than top for stability.

cards.title: uppercase P: Upper Bowl
cards.body: P = H stem + upper bowl. Bowl larger than top of B.
tips: Keep interior counter open and breathing.

cards.title: uppercase R: Diagonal Leg
cards.body: R = P + diagonal leg. Leg is the personality statement.
tips: No visible angle change at junction.

cards.title: uppercase V: Cap Diagonals
cards.body: Sets angle for W, Y, K, A. Heavy left, thin right.

cards.title: uppercase Q: The Tail Signature
cards.body: Q = O + tail. Expressive design signature opportunity.
tips: Tail can be internal or external.
Test spacing in words like Quality.

cards.title: uppercase A: Apex and Crossbar
cards.body: A = inverted V + crossbar. Apex treatment and crossbar position are key design decisions.
tips: Left diagonal is thick (downstroke), right is thin (upstroke).

cards.title: uppercase S: Visual Stability
cards.body: Uppercase S follows lowercase s logic at cap height. Top lobe slightly smaller than bottom.

cards.title: uppercase G: The Bar
cards.body: G = C + horizontal bar/chin. Bar height affects sophistication.
tips: Bar height matches H crossbar or slightly lower.
Curved transition from bottom bowl is common.

cards.title: uppercase K: Cap Diagonals
cards.body: K = H stem + z/v diagonals scaled to cap height.

cards.title: uppercase V: Cap Diagonal Pivot
cards.body: V sets angles for W, Y, K, A. Heavy left, thin right.

cards.title: uppercase W: Double V
cards.body: W = doubled V. Narrow the center for optical rhythm.

cards.title: uppercase Y: Triple Junction
cards.body: Y = V top + I bottom. Junction height is critical.

cards.title: uppercase N: The Diagonal Connector
cards.body: N = two H stems + heavy diagonal. Diagonal must be significantly thicker.
tips: Vertical uprights can be slightly thinner than H.
